ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "logo_id" TEXT /* Unique logo ID assigned to the product */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date the product was approved */,
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the vendor of the product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name and description of the product */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Version of the firmware used in the product */,
  "product_family" TEXT /* Family of products the product belongs to */,
  "product_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the product */,
  "product_image_url" TEXT /* URL to the product image */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the product is currently active */,
  "warranty_period" TEXT /* Warranty period for the product */,
  "last_firmware_update" TEXT /* Date of the last firmware update */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the product was released */,
  "product_specifications" TEXT /* JSON/BLOB field to store technical specifications */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_vendor_id FOREIGN KEY ("vendor_id") REFERENCES vendors ("vendor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_product_family FOREIGN KEY ("product_family") REFERENCES product_families ("family_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "vendors" (
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vendor */,
  "vendor_name" TEXT /* Name of the vendor */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the vendor */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the vendor */,
  "address" TEXT /* Vendor's physical address */,
  "website" TEXT /* Vendor's website URL */,
  "primary_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the primary contact person */,
  "secondary_contact_email" TEXT /* Secondary contact email */,
  "secondary_contact_phone" TEXT /* Secondary contact phone number */,
  "performance_rating" REAL /* Vendor's performance rating */,
  "is_preferred" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the vendor is preferred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vendor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_families" (
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product family */,
  "family_name" TEXT /* Name of the product family */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product family */,
  "family_image_url" TEXT /* URL to the product family image */,
  "family_specifications" TEXT /* JSON/BLOB field to store family-level technical specifications */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the family is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_documents" (
  "document_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the document belongs to */,
  "document_type" TEXT /* Type of document (e.g., datasheet, user manual) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the document */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the document was uploaded */,
  "document_version" TEXT /* Version number of the document */,
  "document_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the document */,
  "uploaded_by" TEXT /* User who uploaded the document */,
  "is_latest" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the document is the latest version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("document_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_documents_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vendor_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vendor making the request */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date the request was made */,
  "request_type" TEXT /* Type of request (e.g., product information, support) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  "request_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the request */,
  "assigned_to" TEXT /* User to whom the request is assigned */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the request */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the request */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding the resolution of the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vendor_requests_vendor_id FOREIGN KEY ("vendor_id") REFERENCES vendors ("vendor_id")
)
